entries.d: To get something by paying money for it
entries.s: I want to _____ a special present for my friend's birthday party on Saturday at the toy shop.

entries.d: To enjoy songs or melodies through your ears
entries.s: He always puts on his wireless headphones to _____ on the long bus ride to school every morning.

entries.d: To put on a pair to see if they look good on you
entries.s: She _____ five different pairs of sunglasses in front of the mirror in the shop to see which looked best on her face.

entries.d: To give money for what you owe
entries.s: After a delicious dinner at the restaurant, my dad _____ at the cash desk with his credit card.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with BILL?

entries.d: To have food or a drink
entries.s: Would you like to _____ something hot or cold at the café — a coffee, a tea, or a slice of cake?

entries.d: To turn your eyes to see something
entries.s: We _____ the beautiful photos from our holiday on my phone together last night after dinner.

entries.d: To have a conversation on your phone
entries.s: Please don't _____ during class with your phone — put it away now and focus on the lesson.

entries.d: When two things happen at the same time by chance
entries.s: We wore exactly the same red dress to the party — what a _____! Neither of us knew in advance.

entries.d: Having a husband or wife
entries.s: My aunt and uncle got _____ last summer in a beautiful garden ceremony with 100 guests.

entries.d: Almost the same but not exactly
entries.s: Your new red handbag and mine are very _____. They are both the same size and colour.

entries.d: To go back to a place, or to give something back
entries.s: Please _____ the library book about dinosaurs before Friday, or you will have to pay a late fee.

entries.d: An expression to show surprise or a strong feeling
entries.s: _____ beautiful sunny day outside with blue sky and warm temperatures! Let's go to the park!

entries.d: A person who writes books or stories
entries.s: This famous children's book _____ has written more than twenty adventure novels for young readers aged 10-14.

entries.d: A person who belongs to a group or club
entries.s: Every _____ of our football club got a gold medal and certificate at the end-of-season party last Friday.

entries.d: A person who travels in a bus, train, car or plane
entries.s: Every _____ on the aeroplane had to fasten their seatbelt tightly before take-off from Vienna airport.

entries.d: To go down below the surface of water
entries.s: The small toy wooden boat started to _____ slowly to the bottom of the swimming pool with water inside it.

entries.d: To stay alive after something dangerous
entries.s: The cat fell from the tall tree but _____ the fall without any serious injuries — it landed on its feet.

entries.d: Not thinking enough about what you do
entries.s: He was _____ with his new phone and dropped it on the hard concrete ground because he wasn't paying attention.

entries.d: A small bag for carrying personal things
entries.s: She put her keys, wallet, phone, and lipstick neatly in her small leather _____ before leaving the house.

entries.d: A polite way to say sorry or to ask someone to repeat something
entries.s: _____. I didn't quite hear what you said. Could you please repeat that a bit louder?

entries.d: To take something that is not yours
entries.s: Someone _____ my expensive red bicycle from the school yard last week without permission, and I never got it back.

entries.d: A person who steals things
entries.s: The police finally caught the _____ who took the old woman's handbag at the train station last Tuesday.

entries.d: The very top point of the Earth
entries.s: It is extremely cold at the _____ where polar bears live on the ice in the far north of Earth.

entries.d: The very bottom point of the Earth
entries.s: Penguins live near the _____ in the icy cold south of Earth where it snows almost every day of the year.

entries.d: Very bad or unpleasant
entries.s: The weather last Saturday was absolutely _____ — it rained heavily all day and we couldn't go outside at all.

entries.d: The door or opening where you go into a place
entries.s: We'll meet at the main _____ of the big shopping centre at three o'clock in the afternoon near the big fountain.

entries.d: To give something to a person in charge
entries.s: Don't forget to _____ your homework to the teacher before Friday morning or you will lose marks.

entries.d: A way to tell someone to wait
entries.s: _____! I need to tie my shoelaces properly before we can start running to catch the bus.

entries.d: To stop bothering someone
entries.s: Please _____! I need some quiet peaceful time by myself to finish my school project without interruptions.

entries.d: To feel happy about something that will happen
entries.s: I am very _____ to the exciting school trip to Rome next week — we're going to see the Colosseum!

entries.d: A line of people waiting for something
entries.s: There was a long _____ of thirty people waiting to buy ice cream at the popular shop in the town square.

entries.d: To stand in a line and wait for your turn
entries.s: We had to _____ in a single file for twenty minutes to get into the popular new museum on opening day.

entries.d: To move your hand to say hello or goodbye
entries.s: She _____ her hand high in the air at her best friend across the crowded playground to get her attention.

entries.d: The day, month and year when you were born
entries.s: Please write your _____ on the form — we need the exact day, month, and year you were born.

entries.d: A way to ask someone to wait a short time
entries.s: _____, I just need to find my house keys before we can leave. Don't go without me.

entries.d: To get what you wanted after trying hard
entries.s: She worked really hard all year and finally _____ her goal of getting straight As on her school report.

entries.d: The sound you make when something is funny
entries.s: His funny joke about the chicken crossing the road got a big loud _____ from the whole class of 30 students.

entries.d: A piece of paper money
entries.s: She found a €20 paper _____ on the ground near the bus stop and handed it to her teacher to find the owner.

entries.d: A part of a hundred, shown with the % sign
entries.s: About 80 _____ of the students in our year group passed the difficult maths test on the first try.

entries.d: A talk given to a group of people
entries.s: The headteacher gave a short 5-minute welcome _____ at the whole-school assembly on the first day of term.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with SPEECH?

entries.d: The raised area where actors or singers perform
entries.s: The rock band walked onto the brightly lit _____ and the huge crowd of 5000 fans cheered loudly.

col.prompt: Which does NOT go with STAGE?

entries.d: To test something to see how it works or if you like it
entries.s: I really want to _____ this new board game — my friend says it looks really fun and interesting.
